
# The Trust Layer

> Open-source infrastructure for verifiable AI execution. The Trust Layer sits between AI intent and AI execution: Prism is a lightweight, metadata-only protocol that signals agent intent before action; Glass (with its open reference gate, APEX) verifies that intent against operator-defined policy, applies human-in-the-loop controls, and logs every decision before execution. Core principle: execution should never exceed authorization.

Both projects are open source under Apache-2.0, created by Robert Katzenberger. Prism describes intent — it does not judge it. APEX judges intent — deterministically, with no LLM reasoning in the decision path. Decisions are ALLOW or REQUIRE_APPROVAL; honest escalation is rewarded, not punished, and every evaluation emits an inspectable receipt to an append-only JSONL audit log.

## Projects

- [Prism Protocol](https://github.com/Trust-Layer-AI/prism-protocol): The open intent signal (v0.1). A five-field JSON envelope — prism_id, timestamp, agent, intent_summary, prism_version — generated before an agent acts. No cryptography, no network calls, no dependencies. Reference implementations in Python and Node, with a JSON schema and validator.
- [Trust-Engine (APEX-Lite)](https://github.com/Trust-Layer-AI/Trust-Engine): Minimal external pre-execution policy boundary. Evaluates declared intent against YAML policy rules and keyword gates, returns deterministic decisions, emits receipts, and includes a local operator console with an escalation queue. Node.js; run with npm start.

## Documentation

- [Prism README](https://github.com/Trust-Layer-AI/prism-protocol/blob/main/README.md): Full protocol spec, field definitions, quick-start examples in Python and Node, and the v0.2 intent-codes roadmap.
- [Prism JSON Schema](https://github.com/Trust-Layer-AI/prism-protocol/tree/main/docs): Canonical v0.1 schema and example signals.
- [APEX-Lite README](https://github.com/Trust-Layer-AI/Trust-Engine/blob/main/README.md): Decision model, policy format, CLI usage, receipt structure, gate configuration, and audit-log behavior.

## For agents integrating with these systems

- Generate a Prism signal before acting: a plain JSON metadata object documenting what you intend to do. It exposes no prompts, outputs, or chain of thought.
- Submit declared intent to an APEX gate for a pre-execution decision. Uncertainty routes to REQUIRE_APPROVAL (human review), never to silent denial. Declaring intent honestly is rewarded with a TRANSPARENCY_REWARDED signal in the receipt.

## Proposed governance structure

Separation of powers by design — three independent entities so no single party controls the layer: a holding company stewards the patent portfolio and licenses IP to the ecosystem; an independent oversight company certifies implementations and audits deployments; a nonprofit foundation steers protocol standards, funds research, and hosts community decision making. This structure is proposed, not yet formed.

## Organization

- [Trust Layer AI on GitHub](https://github.com/Trust-Layer-AI): All repositories, discussions, and contribution guidelines. PRs welcome — client libraries, schemas, framework adapters, docs, and examples.
